mnemonics.title: Population vs Sample: 'P' and 'S' Rule
mnemonics.content: Parameters describe Populations (both start with P, use Greek letters like mu and sigma). Statistics describe Samples (both start with S, use Roman letters like x-bar and s). Remember: 'P-P-Greek' and 'S-S-Roman' to never confuse them.

mnemonics.title: Two Branches of Inference: 'EH' - Estimate then Hypothesize
mnemonics.content: Statistical inference has two branches: Estimation (E) and Hypothesis testing (H). Think 'EH?' as if you are questioning the population. First you Estimate the parameter (point or interval), then you test a Hypothesis about it. EH = Estimation + Hypothesis testing.

mnemonics.title: Sources of Bias: 'SRNM' - Sampling Requires No Mistakes
mnemonics.content: The four main sources of bias are: Selection bias, Response bias, Non-response bias, and Measurement bias. Remember the acronym SRNM: 'Sampling Requires No Mistakes.' Each letter maps to a source of bias that can ruin your inference if not controlled.
